3.happening: Count the {len(values)} array slots that describe the tree.
3.why: We need the size before building node objects.
3.invariant: n is the length of the description array.

4.happening: Start an empty list of real tree nodes.
4.why: We build node objects the visualization can draw.
4.invariant: nodes holds every non-null value as a node.

5.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.
5.why: The recursion fetches nodes by id in O(1).
5.invariant: by_id[i] is the node stored at slot i.

6.why: It records each node as unseen, on-stack, or done — that drives the colors.

7.happening: Loop over every slot to initialize its state.
7.invariant: Slots below i already have a state.

9.happening: Start the child-slot walker just past the root.
9.why: Children are handed out in array order from slot 1.
9.invariant: child is the next unassigned slot.

10.happening: Loop over every slot to build the nodes.
10.why: Each real value becomes a node linked to its children.
10.invariant: Slots below i are already built.

11.why: A null slot is a placeholder, not a node.

12.happening: Create node {i} with value {values[i]} and empty child links.
12.why: Children are filled in from the following slots.
12.invariant: A fresh node starts with no children.

13.happening: Is there a slot left to be the left child?
13.why: Children come from the next slots in order.
13.invariant: child points at the candidate left child.

14.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
14.invariant: A null candidate leaves the left child empty.

15.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s left child.
15.why: It is the next real node in level order.
15.invariant: left now points at a real child id.

16.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

17.happening: Is there a slot left to be the right child?
17.why: The right child is the slot after the left.
17.invariant: child points at the candidate right child.

18.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
18.invariant: A null candidate leaves the right child empty.

19.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s right child.
19.why: It is the next real node in level order.
19.invariant: right now points at a real child id.

20.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

21.happening: Add the finished node to the tree.
21.why: It now has its value and child links.
21.invariant: nodes grows by one built node.

22.happening: Register node {i} under its id for fast lookup.
22.why: The recursion fetches nodes by id.
22.invariant: by_id maps every id to its node.

23.happening: The root is slot 0 (or nothing if the tree is empty).
23.invariant: root identifies where the recursion begins.

24.happening: Recursively invert the whole tree from the root.
24.why: Inverting = swap a node's children, then invert each subtree.
24.invariant: After this call every node's children are swapped.

25.why: We serialize the inverted tree to return it.
25.invariant: result will hold the answer array.

26.happening: Start a queue to walk the inverted tree.
26.why: A breadth-first walk emits nodes in level order.

27.happening: The tree is non-empty — seed the walk with the root.
27.why: Level order starts at the root.

27.happening: The tree is empty — nothing to serialize.
27.why: An empty tree serializes to an empty array.

28.happening: Enqueue the root id to begin the walk.
28.invariant: The root leads the level-order output.

29.happening: Ids remain — emit the next one.
29.why: We serialize until the walk queue empties.
29.invariant: Every reachable node is emitted once.

29.happening: The walk queue is empty — serialization is done.
29.invariant: result now holds every node in level order.

30.happening: Dequeue the next id from the front.
30.why: Breadth-first emits the earliest-queued id next.

31.happening: This slot is a missing child.
31.why: Level order records holes as null.
31.invariant: Missing children still take a slot.

31.happening: This id points at a real node.
31.invariant: A real id has a value and two child links.

32.happening: Emit null for the missing child.
32.why: The level-order format keeps holes explicit.

34.happening: Emit this node's value into the result.
34.why: Level order lists values top-down, left-to-right.

35.happening: Queue this node's left child to emit next.
35.why: Children follow their parent in level order.

36.happening: Queue this node's right child after the left.
36.why: Right follows left within a level.

37.happening: The output ends in a null — trim it.
37.why: Level order omits trailing null holes.
37.invariant: No trailing nulls remain in the answer.

37.happening: No trailing null to trim — the answer is clean.
37.why: The array already ends in a real value (or is empty).
37.invariant: result is the final level-order form.

38.why: Trailing holes are not part of the level-order answer.

39.happening: Return the inverted tree in level-order form: {result}.
39.why: Every node's children have been swapped and serialized.

42.happening: A missing child — nothing to invert here.
42.why: The recursion bottoms out at absent children.
Every real node has its children swapped.
42.invariant: This node is about to be processed.

43.happening: Return immediately for the missing child.
43.why: There is nothing below to swap.

44.happening: Mark node {node_id} active — its frame is on the call stack.
44.why: Amber nodes are the current root-to-node path being processed.

45.why: We need the old left before overwriting it.
45.invariant: left holds the pre-swap left child.

46.why: We need the old right before overwriting it.
46.invariant: right holds the pre-swap right child.

47.happening: Set node {node_id}'s left to its old right child.
47.why: This is half of swapping the two children.
47.invariant: The left link now points where right did.

48.happening: Set node {node_id}'s right to its old left child.
48.why: The two children are now swapped — this node is mirrored.
48.invariant: This node's subtrees have traded sides.

49.happening: Recurse into node {node_id}'s new left subtree.
49.why: Depth-first: fully mirror one side before the other.
49.invariant: The left subtree is inverted before the right.

50.happening: Recurse into node {node_id}'s new right subtree.
50.why: Its whole subtree is inverted in turn.
50.invariant: Both subtrees are inverted after these calls.

51.happening: Both subtrees done — mark node {node_id} fully inverted.
51.why: Blue nodes are finished; the frame is about to pop.

52.invariant: Control unwinds one level up the stack.

init.happening: Building the tree from the level-order array.
init.why: We need node objects and links before inverting.
init.invariant: Nodes pop in and connect to their parents.

recurse.happening: Descending into a subtree with a recursive call.
recurse.why: DFS inverts each subtree in turn.
recurse.invariant: The call stack traces the current path.

check.why: Null slots and empty walks are handled specially.

move.happening: Unwinding a completed call back up the tree.
move.why: A finished subtree returns control to its parent.

update.why: Inverting a node is swapping its two child links.
update.invariant: State and links reflect the work done.

loop.happening: Sweeping the inverted tree to serialize it.
loop.why: A breadth-first walk emits level-order output.

return.happening: Returning the inverted tree in level-order form.
return.why: Every node has been swapped and serialized.
